source_jobs.title: Applications Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Overview.ai http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is one of the fastest growing US startups in the AI space. Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing—solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We’re a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We’re growing extremely fast. Our customers love the product because it works—high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).



Why this role is different

Our demand is exploding in the possible way, and you will help deliver that value to customers and help us grow even more.

You’ll lead a team of applications engineers (vision, controls, mechanical) and technicians to deliver end-to-end deployments—from “PO signed” to “system stable in production” to “human inspectors removed.” This is the role where execution becomes revenue, and revenue becomes the next wave of growth.

If you’ve thrived in high-tempo manufacturing environments, you’ll recognize the pace—and you’ll enjoy the satisfaction of building a world-class deployment engine instead of getting buried by firefighting.

What you’ll own

1) Deployment outcomes at scale

 - Own the delivery of deployments across multiple factories and lines: schedule, staffing, readiness, execution, stabilization.

 - Drive deployments to the finish line: stable inspections, acceptance criteria met, and clear handoff to customer operations.

 - Build repeatable playbooks that reduce time-to-production and increase customer self-sufficiency.

2) Team leadership (hire, train, operate)

 - Lead and grow a high-performance field team: applications engineers + technicians across regions.

 - Set standards for execution: daily/weekly cadence, escalation paths, documentation, and quality bars.

 - Coach your team to be fast, calm, and effective under pressure—especially when production is on the line.

3) Customer leadership (scope, expectations, urgency)

 - Be the on-the-ground leader with customer stakeholders: process, quality, automation, and factory management.

 - Manage scope ruthlessly but professionally: protect outcomes, prevent scope creep, and keep the project moving.

 - Drive urgency: align customer resources, line access, sample parts, and decision-making so deployments don’t stall.

4) Process and systems building

 - Create the operational infrastructure that makes deployment scale:
   
   - onboarding + training frameworks, checklists, commissioning templates, project dashboards, post-mortems, and continuous improvement loops

 - Track what matters: deployment cycle time, blockers, time-to-stability, acceptance rate, expansion readiness.

5) Product feedback that changes the roadmap

 - Translate field pain into product improvements that make deployments faster and easier:
   
   - better diagnostics, clearer workflows, installation standards, tooling, documentation, and “self-serve” capabilities

 - Work closely with global engineering and product to push changes that remove recurring friction.

 - 

What success looks like

 - Deployments become predictable: faster ramp, fewer surprises, clear ownership.

 - Customers trust Overview.ai http://Overview.ai to scale: more lines, more factories, more expansion.

 - Your team operates like an elite unit: strong execution, great communication, high morale.

 - The product gets better because you turned field reality into roadmap wins



Who you are

Required

 - Proven track record leading complex, multi-site technical deployments in manufacturing environments

 - Strong program/project management ability: you can run multiple workstreams, prioritize ruthlessly, and keep people aligned

 - Experience leading teams (hiring, training, perform

source_jobs.title: Vision Sales Engineer - San Francisco
source_jobs.description:
VISION SALES ENGINEER

Location: California (field-first; hybrid when not onsite)
Territory: California (based in San Francisco)
Travel: 60–80% (factory-heavy)
Type: Full-time




ABOUT OVERVIEW.AI http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing, solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We're a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We're one of the fastest-growing industrial AI companies in the world. We grew ~700% last year, tripled headcount, and expect to double or triple again as demand keeps accelerating —because the product works in production: high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).




THE OPPORTUNITY (WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT)

This is not a cold-start, 0→1 sales role. We already have major customers, executive buy-in, and proven deployments running in real factories today. Your job is to take that momentum and multiply it across California:

 - Turn warm introductions into live factory projects fast

 - Convert first-station wins into repeatable rollouts across lines and sites

 - Drive urgency and close expansions that don't "drift to next quarter"

 - Unblock real-world constraints (technical, operational, organizational) that slow velocity

You'll be selling at the exact moment manufacturing leadership is demanding AI outcomes. This is one of the easiest and most tangible ways for a factory to "enter AI" because it directly improves yield, scrap, throughput, and labor.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN


1) TERRITORY REVENUE OWNERSHIP (CALIFORNIA)

 - Own pipeline, forecasting, and revenue outcomes for California

 - Start with hunting - win new inspection points and new lines - then build into expansion as your accounts grow

 - Turn customer success into aggressive expansion across factories, lines, and inspection points

 - You'll be dropped into meaningful accounts early so you can hit the ground running - top logos, real budgets, real urgency


2) TECHNICAL, CONSULTATIVE SELLING (VISION + AUTOMATION)

 - Run high-signal discovery with Quality, Process, Automation, and Ops

 - Understand how customers currently solve inspection, where incumbents fail, and how to win

 - Position Overview clearly vs incumbents using real deltas: accuracy, deployability, operator usability, and platform capabilities

 - Partner with applications + engineering to propose practical deployment plans that win


3) CREATE URGENCY AND REMOVE FRICTION

 - Drive timelines: next steps, milestones, line access, data collection, acceptance criteria, decision makers

 - Identify blockers early (process readiness, resourcing, line access, mechanical stability, integration constraints) and push them to resolution

 - Own executive updates and communication cadence to keep deals moving


4) OPERATE LIKE A CLOSER

 - Build relationships that hold: plant champions → regional leaders → global decision makers

 - Negotiate and close with speed and precision

 - Be relentless about follow-up and execution—no "maybe next quarter" drift

 - If you own a major account, you get credit and commission for all sites in your territory—regardless of how much help we bring in




WHY WE WIN (AND WHY SELLING OVERVIEW IS DIFFERENT)

 - Industry-leading AI models → higher accuracy on real-world variation

 - Edge-first + production-ready → deterministic performance in factories

 - A feature set competitors don't have → you can win on clear deltas (the easiest kind of selling)

 - Agentic workflows → deploying cameras is easier than ever, which shortens cycles and accelerates expansions This is "delta selling" with a product customers genuinely want.

The key to winning isn't slick talk—it's becoming a product expert

source_jobs.title: Senior Field Engineer (Controls)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OVERVIEW.AI http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing—solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We’re a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We’re growing extremely fast. Our customers love the product because it works—high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).



WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING

 - Real production ownership: You’re not “support.” You own uptime and line performance where it matters—on the factory floor.
   

 - Hard problems, high signal: Intermittent I/O, timing jitter, noisy signals, flaky networks, multi-trigger edge cases, cycle-time constraints—this is what you’ll solve.
   

 - High learning + high leverage: You’ll deepen elite controls skills while working alongside world-class AI, embedded, and firmware teams to ship permanent fixes into the product.
   

 - Talent density: You’ll be surrounded by fast, intense people who execute. Your work will be taken seriously and acted on quickly.
   

 - High travel, high autonomy: You’ll be trusted as the senior technical owner across multiple sites.
   


WHAT YOU’LL OWN


CONTROLS + LINE INTEGRATION (CORE OF THE JOB)

 - Integrate Overview.ai http://Overview.ai systems into automation lines and make them rock-solid.
   

 - Commission and troubleshoot PLC-to-device communication across common industrial protocols:
   
   
   - EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, Modbus, OPC UA (and real-world variants)
     

 - Diagnose and eliminate timing and triggering issues:
   
   
   - double triggers, missed triggers, trigger bounce, incorrect edge detection, latency/jitter, inconsistent handshakes
     

 - Debug inconsistent network communication:
   
   
   - packet loss, unstable links, IP conflicts, switch configuration issues, grounding/noise coupling, cable quality, EMI, misconfigured QoS/VLANs (where applicable)
     

 - Work with PLC logic and machine sequencing to ensure correct system behavior:
   
   
   - interpret and modify ladder logic / structured text (as needed) to stabilize integration and performance
     

 - Build repeatable integration standards:
   
   
   - wiring patterns, sensor conditioning recommendations, network topologies, validated PLC templates, commissioning checklists
     


ELECTRICAL + SIGNAL INTEGRITY DEBUGGING (HANDS-ON)

 - Troubleshoot sensors, discrete I/O, and signal conditioning in real factory environments:
   
   
   - NPN/PNP sensors, pull-ups/pull-downs, filtering/debounce, shielding/grounding, noise mitigation
     

 - Use multimeters and oscilloscopes to diagnose:
   
   
   - voltage levels, signal bounce, timing alignment, interference, poor grounding, intermittent connections
     

 - Identify root cause quickly and implement robust fixes—not temporary patches.
   


FIELD LEADERSHIP + CUSTOMER TRUST

 - Be the senior technical point of contact on-site: calm, decisive, and structured under pressure.
   

 - Train customer automation engineers and technicians on best practices for operation and first-line troubleshooting.
   

 - Capture high-quality issue reports for HQ engineering:
   
   
   - reproduction steps, logs, electrical measurements, network symptoms, PLC state, and recommended fixes
     

 - Drive feedback loops that result in real product improvements (tooling, diagnostics, reliability features).
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR


REQUIRED

 - Deep PLC / controls engineering experience (you can independently commission and debug automation lines)
   

 - 5+ years in controls / automation / field engineering in high-volume manufacturing environments
   

 - Hands-on expertise with industrial networks and protocols:
   
   
   - EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, Mo

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OVERVIEW AI

Overview AI is a VC-funded, YC-backed company building edge AI vision systems for manufacturing quality inspection. Our all-in-one, edge-enabled cameras are used by leading global manufacturers including Tesla, SpaceX, Amphenol, and others.

We’ve seen rapid growth over the past two years and are now investing in highly talented generalist talent that can support GTM structure, clarity, and scale. This role is designed for someone who wants to operate at the intersection of strategy and execution, is a real doer, excels at bringing structure to chaos, and has a real ownership mindset.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a high-ownership GTM Operations Lead to work closely with sales leadership and founders to help scale our go-to-market motion, primarily in the US and Mexico. We are trying to triple or more revenue in this region and this role will work alongside regional leadership and the founders focused on strategic and operational elements to achieve this goal.

This is a hands-on operator role. You’ll help decide strategy, where to focus/prioritize efforts, build the tools and collateral that enable sales, clean up and own our CRM and reporting, and support hiring and onboarding as the team grows.

If you enjoy ambiguous problems, building from scratch, and working directly with leadership, this role is for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


GTM STRATEGY & FOCUS

 - Help identify where to focus vs. deprioritize across markets, verticals, territories, and customer segments

 - Translate leadership input into execution plans, metrics, and progress tracking

 - Support decision-making with analysis, memos, and structured thinking

 - Run cross-functional experiments, such as targeting a specific application, including working with marketing, outbound sales, and other teams to drive strong sales traction


SALES ENABLEMENT & COLLATERAL (HANDS-ON)

 - Become deeply familiar with our AI cameras and use cases

 - Personally create and iterate on:
   
   - Sales decks and narratives
   
   - Case studies and ROI materials
   
   - Playbooks by vertical or customer type

 - Support and potentially lead internal sales trainings


REVENUE OPERATIONS & ANALYTICS

 - Support and help lead our HubSpot revamp

 - Own CRM architecture, hygiene, dashboards, and reporting

 - Build reporting used for:
   
   - Weekly pipeline reviews
   
   - Founder and leadership updates
   
   - Identifying long-term GTM improvements


HIRING & ORG DESIGN SUPPORT

 - Help define roles and hiring plans for sales and GTM functions

 - Support interviewing and onboarding

 - Contribute to evolving GTM org structure as the company scales


CUSTOMER & PARTNER EXPOSURE

 - Join customer calls, partner meetings, and internal GTM discussions

 - Build firsthand understanding of how deals are won and lost

 - Potential to expand and work on experimental sales channels




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience in a startup or other high paced and ambiguous environment

 - Proven ability to work cross-functionally

 - Strong operational and execution mindset, strong bias to action

 - Ability to design structure and process without over-bureaucratizing

 - Willingness to travel to manufacturing sites in the US and potentially internationally for specific projects

 - Technical background / degree / experience or willingness to dig deeper into camera technology




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in technical domains (engineering, hardware, AI, manufacturing, automation)

 - Experience working and interfacing across multiple countries

 - Exposure to building revenue operations infrastructure from scratch




WHY JOIN

 - High-impact role with direct founder partnership

 - Opportunity to shape how the company scales globally

 - Ownership over systems, team, and outcomes

 - Work alongside world-class engineering and product leaders

source_jobs.title: Senior Embedded Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR EMBEDDED SOFTWARE ENGINEER



ABOUT OVERVIEW

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is transforming manufacturing quality control with advanced edge-enabled AI vision system that combines cutting-edge deep learning with ease of use and quick setup. Our system handles a wide array of inspection tasks—from subtle assembly verification to defect detection and orientation checks—delivering real-time, high-accuracy inspections that seamlessly integrate into existing production lines.

Trusted by leading manufacturers like Ford, Honda, Toyota, SpaceX, Milliken, and Flex-N-Gate, our cameras enables faster throughput, reduced scrap, and lower inspection costs—without the complexity and expense of traditional vision systems.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Senior Embedded Systems Software Engineer with strong Embedded Linux experience to join our engineering team. You will design, build, and maintain the software that powers our NVIDIA Jetson–based edge AI cameras — including Python application code, system services, OTA update mechanisms, networking, and device reliability.

This is a hands-on engineering role focused on Linux systems and product software running on resource-constrained devices. You will not be working on MCU firmware or low-level hardware bring-up. Instead, you’ll operate across the OS and application stack to ensure our camera systems are robust, secure, and easy to deploy at scale.

If you enjoy building software for real hardware, solving complex debugging challenges, and owning features end-to-end, we would love to speak with you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Develop and maintain system-level and application-level software for reliability in the field for our edge AI devices

 - Implement and own OTA for our deployed device fleet

 - Write Python application code for device control, edge logic, monitoring, and data flows

 - Work with C/C++ components for performance-critical functionality

 - Debug Linux systems involving multiple services, containers, and custom applications

 - Tune performance across the stack: kernel, services, containers, and user applications

 - Use Docker containers for packaging and deploying edge software components

 - Collaborate with hardware vendors to diagnose and resolve system-level issues

 - Work with backend/API teams to maintain reliable device–server communication

 - Mentor the team through code review, coaching, and general feedback




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related field

 - 5-7+ years of experience in Linux-based embedded systems or systems software

 - Solid C++ skills in a Linux environment and/or Python development experience

 - Experience with SBC or Embedded Linux platforms

 - Understanding of networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, routing, TLS/HTTPS, certificates)

 - Experience debugging Linux applications and services (systemd, logs, containers)

 - Experience with Docker containerization

 - Strong problem-solving skills and independent ownership mindset

 - Clear communication and collaboration skills




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience implementing OTA systems or device-update workflows

 - NodeRED, Flask, or REST API development

 - Industrial automation background (PLC ladder logic, Structured Text)

 - Industrial protocols: EtherNet/IP, Profinet, Modbus, RS232, RS485, CANbus

 - Experience with OpenCV, GStreamer, or real-time video processing

 - Experience with FTP/SFTP/SMB, NTP synchronization, or device-to-server messaging

 - Experience with fleet management of edge devices




WHY JOIN

 - Build core systems that directly impact real-world manufacturing

 - High ownership and autonomy

 - Work closely with hardware, AI, and customer-facing teams

 - Join a fast growing, fast moving and profitable startup

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OVERVIEW.AI http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing—solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We’re a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We’re growing extremely fast. Our customers love the product because it works—high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).



THE MISSION

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is building the most powerful edge AI vision platform for manufacturing—deployed on real production lines, not in lab demos. Our AI is only as good as the images we feed it. That’s why this role exists.

We’re hiring a top-tier Vision Applications Engineer in China—someone who can walk up to a nasty inspection problem (reflective metal, transparent plastic, motion blur, awkward geometry, cramped mounting, unstable presentation) and immediately start forming a lighting + optics strategy that makes the problem solvable. You’ll be the person customers remember: the one who makes “impossible” inspections suddenly look obvious.

This role is vision-first. You don’t need to be a controls engineer. You do need to be an exceptional optical/lighting problem solver who can also own field execution: customer communication, on-site deployments, training, and project delivery.




WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING

 - Solve the hardest vision problems in the world: The customers we work with bring problems that break traditional machine vision. You’ll be in the deep end.

 - Work with cutting-edge AI + real factories: You’ll turn great imaging into great AI outcomes—and see the impact immediately on yield and downtime.

 - High autonomy, high trust: You’ll be the expert in the room, leading technical strategy and owning outcomes end-to-end.

 - Win against incumbents: You’ll know the competitive landscape (Cognex, Keyence, HALCON, Hikvision, etc.) and use that knowledge to help us close deals and expand deployments.

 - Travel + variety: New lines, new parts, new weird optical phenomena—this role stays interesting.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL OWN


1) VISION STRATEGY: OPTICS + LIGHTING (CORE OF THE ROLE)

You will design and iterate the imaging solution that makes inspection reliable:

 - Select and tune lighting for challenging surfaces and geometries:
   
   - bright field / dark field, coaxial, dome, diffuse, low-angle, structured light, line lights, spot/point, backlight, multisource setups
   
   - polarization (cross/parallel), glare suppression, specular management
   
   - strobed lighting vs continuous, high-speed exposure strategies, motion-freezing
   
   - “2.5D” techniques: photometric-style approaches, multi-angle illumination, gradient/shape enhancement

 - Select and deploy lenses and optical elements:
   
   - telecentric, macro, high-NA, long working distance, wide-angle vs narrow, Scheimpflug (tilt) where appropriate
   
   - filters (bandpass, long-pass/short-pass), polarizers, diffusers, beam splitters
   
   - mirrors/prisms for impossible mounting angles and constrained spaces

 - Push beyond visible light when needed:
   
   - IR / NIR / SWIR (and other spectral strategies), multispectral approaches, fluorescence-style thinking where relevant

 - Diagnose and solve “weird” vision failure modes:
   
   - reflectivity, transparency, subsurface features, moiré, aliasing, lens flare/ghosting, vibration blur, rolling shutter artifacts, shadowing/occlusion, contamination drift

The bar: you can often tease out a workable imaging approach just by looking at the part + environment, then validate it quickly in the field.

 - High learning + high leverage: You’ll deepen elite controls skills while working alongside world-class AI, embedded, and firmware teams to ship permanent fixes into the product.

 - Talent density: You’ll be surrounded by fast, intense people who execute. Your work will be taken seriously and acted on quickly.

 - High travel, high autonomy: You’ll be trusted as the senior technical owner across multiple sites.


WHAT YOU’LL OWN


CONTROLS + LINE INTEGRATION (CORE OF THE JOB)

 - Integrate Overview.ai http://Overview.ai systems into automation lines and make them rock-solid.

 - Commission and troubleshoot PLC-to-device communication across common industrial protocols:
   
   - EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, Modbus, OPC UA (and real-world variants)

 - Diagnose and eliminate timing and triggering issues:
   
   - double triggers, missed triggers, trigger bounce, incorrect edge detection, latency/jitter, inconsistent handshakes

 - Debug inconsistent network communication:
   
   - packet loss, unstable links, IP conflicts, switch configuration issues, grounding/noise coupling, cable quality, EMI, misconfigured QoS/VLANs (where applicable)

 - Work with PLC logic and machine sequencing to ensure correct system behavior:
   
   - interpret and modify ladder logic / structured text (as needed) to stabilize integration and performance

 - Build repeatable integration standards:
   
   - wiring patterns, sensor conditioning recommendations, network topologies, validated PLC templates, commissioning checklists


ELECTRICAL + SIGNAL INTEGRITY DEBUGGING (HANDS-ON)

 - Troubleshoot sensors, discrete I/O, and signal conditioning in real factory environments:
   
   - NPN/PNP sensors, pull-ups/pull-downs, filtering/debounce, shielding/grounding, noise mitigation

 - Use multimeters and oscilloscopes to diagnose:
   
   - voltage levels, signal bounce, timing alignment, interference, poor grounding, intermittent connections

 - Identify root cause quickly and implement robust fixes—not temporary patches.


FIELD LEADERSHIP + CUSTOMER TRUST

 - Be the senior technical point of contact on-site: calm, decisive, and structured under pressure.

 - Train customer automation engineers and technicians on best practices for operation and first-line troubleshooting.

 - Capture high-quality issue reports for HQ engineering:
   
   - reproduction steps, logs, electrical measurements, network symptoms, PLC state, and recommended fixes

 - Drive feedback loops that result in real product improvements (tooling, diagnostics, reliability features).


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR


REQUIRED

 - Deep PLC / controls engineering experience (you can independently commission and debug automation lines)

 - 5+ years in controls / automation / field engineering in high-volume manufacturing environments

 - Hands-on expertise with industrial networks and protocols:
   
   - EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, Modbus, OPC UA (practical troubleshooting, not just theory)

 - Strong electrical debugging ability:
   
   - multimeter + oscilloscop

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OVERVIEW.AI http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing—solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We’re a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We’re growing extremely fast. Our customers love the product because it works—high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).



THE MISSION

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is building the most powerful edge AI vision platform for manufacturing—deployed on real production lines, not in lab demos. Our AI is only as good as the images we feed it. That’s why this role exists.

We’re hiring a top-tier Vision Applications Engineer in China—someone who can walk up to a nasty inspection problem (reflective metal, transparent plastic, motion blur, awkward geometry, cramped mounting, unstable presentation) and immediately start forming a lighting + optics strategy that makes the problem solvable. You’ll be the person customers remember: the one who makes “impossible” inspections suddenly look obvious.

This role is vision-first. You don’t need to be a controls engineer. You do need to be an exceptional optical/lighting problem solver who can also own field execution: customer communication, on-site deployments, training, and project delivery.




WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING

 - Solve the hardest vision problems in the world: The customers we work with bring problems that break traditional machine vision. You’ll be in the deep end.

 - Work with cutting-edge AI + real factories: You’ll turn great imaging into great AI outcomes—and see the impact immediately on yield and downtime.

 - High autonomy, high trust: You’ll be the expert in the room, leading technical strategy and owning outcomes end-to-end.

 - Win against incumbents: You’ll know the competitive landscape (Cognex, Keyence, HALCON, Hikvision, etc.) and use that knowledge to help us close deals and expand deployments.

 - Travel + variety: New lines, new parts, new weird optical phenomena—this role stays interesting.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL OWN


1) VISION STRATEGY: OPTICS + LIGHTING (CORE OF THE ROLE)

You will design and iterate the imaging solution that makes inspection reliable:

 - Select and tune lighting for challenging surfaces and geometries:
   
   - bright field / dark field, coaxial, dome, diffuse, low-angle, structured light, line lights, spot/point, backlight, multisource setups
   
   - polarization (cross/parallel), glare suppression, specular management
   
   - strobed lighting vs continuous, high-speed exposure strategies, motion-freezing
   
   - “2.5D” techniques: photometric-style approaches, multi-angle illumination, gradient/shape enhancement

 - Select and deploy lenses and optical elements:
   
   - telecentric, macro, high-NA, long working distance, wide-angle vs narrow, Scheimpflug (tilt) where appropriate
   
   - filters (bandpass, long-pass/short-pass), polarizers, diffusers, beam splitters
   
   - mirrors/prisms for impossible mounting angles and constrained spaces

 - Push beyond visible light when needed:
   
   - IR / NIR / SWIR (and other spectral strategies), multispectral approaches, fluorescence-style thinking where relevant

 - Diagnose and solve “weird” vision failure modes:
   
   - reflectivity, transparency, subsurface features, moiré, aliasing, lens flare/ghosting, vibration blur, rolling shutter artifacts, shadowing/occlusion, contamination drift

The bar: you can often tease out a workable imaging approach just by looking at the part + environment, then validate it quickly in the field.




2) PROJECT OWNERSHIP: DEPLOY, STABILIZE, SCALE

 - Own customer deployments end-to-end: planning, on-site execution, training, follow-through.

 - D

source_jobs.title: Application Engineer
source_jobs.description:
🏢 COMPANY OVERVIEW

Overview.ai is transforming manufacturing by delivering advanced AI-powered vision inspection systems that ensure exceptional quality and efficiency. Our mission is to make manufacturing smarter, more precise, and scalable through innovative computer vision and robust, user-friendly software.



Founded by Silicon Valley engineers and former Tesla leaders, we support industry innovators like Tesla, SpaceX, Honda, and NVIDIA. We’re rapidly expanding and focused on providing the industry’s most effective and easy-to-deploy AI vision solutions.




📋 POSITION OVERVIEW

We are looking for a talented and adaptable Application Engineer to become our inaugural team member in Hanoi, Vietnam. In this role, you will maintain and optimize over 100 AI vision inspection cameras installed across customer facilities in Hanoi and assist with new production line deployments.

This role calls for strong industrial automation expertise, a problem-solving mindset, and the ability to work independently while managing multiple customer sites. As the dedicated local expert, you will act as the primary technical contact for Hanoi customers, ensuring swift and reliable support as well as optimal system performance.




🚀 KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


SYSTEM MAINTENANCE & SUPPORT

 - Maintain and optimize 100+ Overview.ai vision inspection cameras across Hanoi customer sites

 - Provide rapid troubleshooting to minimize production downtime

 - Perform regular preventive maintenance and system health checks

 - Oversee and coordinate a small team of contractors for routine tasks


TECHNICAL DEPLOYMENT & INTEGRATION

 - Install, configure, and troubleshoot Overview.ai vision inspection systems on new production lines

 - Work with various PLC platforms and protocols, including Ethernet/IP, Profinet, Modbus, and OPC UA

 - Ensure seamless integration of AI inspection systems with factory automation equipment

 - Use multimeters and oscilloscopes to diagnose and fix electrical and signal quality issues


CUSTOMER SUPPORT & TRAINING

 - Deliver on-site technical support and guidance to customers

 - Conduct training programs for customer staff on operation and basic troubleshooting

 - Build trusted partnerships with factory teams, production management, and technical staff

 - Document and report recurring issues to enhance product features


SYSTEM OPTIMIZATION

 - Address technical challenges such as double triggering, camera response problems, or lighting inconsistencies

 - Adjust PLC ladder logic as needed to resolve integration issues

 - Refine inspection parameters to decrease false rejects while ensuring high detection accuracy

 - Collaborate with headquarters engineering teams for system improvements




🎓 QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Automation, or a related discipline

 - 3+ years of experience in automation, with proficiency across multiple PLC systems

 - Practical experience troubleshooting electrical circuits using diagnostic tools (multimeters, oscilloscopes)

 - Strong understanding of electrical concepts, including NPN/PNP sensor technology and signal conditioning

 - Familiarity with PLC ladder logic and ability to make necessary modifications

 - Experience managing contractors or small technical teams


TECHNICAL SKILLS

 - Proficiency with industrial communication protocols (Ethernet/IP, Profinet, Modbus, OPC UA)

 - Capability to modify circuits (e.g., resistors, capacitors) to resolve signal problems

 - Knowledge of machine vision lighting and triggering systems

 - Excellent practical diagnostic and problem-solving skills


LANGUAGE & COMMUNICATION

 - Working proficiency in English for technical documentation and coordination with HQ

 - Strong communication skills for client and team interactions

 - Mandarin language proficiency is an extremely strong plus

 - Vietnamese language proficiency is a strong plus, but not required




🌟 PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

source_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
THE MISSION

We build industrial AI cameras that power factories across the US, China and 15+ countries around the world.. On our engineering team, we are trying to balance fast speed to market and extremely high reliability needs. If our cameras go down, production stops. We are looking for our first Technical Program Manager to help organize a growing engineering team in Taipei and bridge the gap between the Taipei engineering hub, our US headquarters, and our global hardware partners.



Why this role?

 - High Impact: You aren't just tracking tickets; you are defining how we scale from 2 products to 4.

 - Direct Access: Direct access and frequent updates to the company’s executive team. You’ll have the autonomy to build the TPM function from scratch.

 - Concrete Results: Your work directly ensures thousands of cameras run smoothly on factory floors worldwide.



What You’ll Do

 - Drive Cross-Functional Programs: Own the end-to-end delivery of hardware and software projects (e.g., custom camera builds with Advantech, Jetpack 6 rollouts).

 - Manage Hardware Partners: Hold ODMs and vendors accountable to design milestones, prototype schedules, and lead-time planning.

 - Ship Software: Lead field release programs, coordinating staged rollouts and maintenance windows with our applications team.

 - Be the Face of Engineering: Join technical deep-dives and QBRs with APAC customers. You’ll translate customer needs into engineering reality.

 - Connect the Regions: Act as the "connective tissue" between our teams in Taipei, China, and San Francisco.



What We’re Looking For

 - Experience: 5–8 years in TPM or Engineering Project Management (A foundation in software project management, with Hardware/Embedded experience as well)

 - Vendor Management: A track record of pushing ODMs/vendors to deliver on time without sacrificing quality.

 - Technical Literacy: Ability to read Datasheets and BOMs. You don’t need to be an EE, but you must be credible when talking to engineers.

 - Communication: Fluent Mandarin and strong professional English are required to navigate our global offices.

 - Field Ready: Willingness to travel to China/customer sites (~once every two months).



Nice to Haves

 - Experience with NVIDIA Jetson, x86, or Embedded Linux.

 - Background working with Advantech, Adlink, or similar partners.

 - Experience being the first TPM in a high-growth startup.

This role is vision-first. You don’t need to be a controls engineer. You do need to be an exceptional optical/lighting problem solver who can also own field execution: customer communication, on-site deployments, training, and project delivery.

 


WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING

● Solve the hardest vision problems in the world: The customers we work with bring problems that break traditional machine vision. You’ll be in the deep end.


● Work with cutting-edge AI + real factories: You’ll turn great imaging into great AI outcomes—and see the impact immediately on yield and downtime.


● High autonomy, high trust: You’ll be the expert in the room, leading technical strategy and owning outcomes end-to-end.

○ “2.5D” techniques: photometric-style approaches, multi-angle illumination, gradient/shape enhancement


● Select and deploy lenses and optical elements:


○ telecentric, macro, high-NA, long working distance, wide-angle vs narrow, Scheimpflug (tilt) where appropriate


○ filters (bandpass, long-pass/short-pass), polarizers, diffusers, beam splitters


○ mirrors/prisms for impossible mounting angles and constrained spaces


● Push beyond visible light when needed:


○ IR / NIR / SWIR (and other spectral strategies), multispectral approaches, fluorescence-style thinking where relevant

● Drive fast iteration cycles: image capture → lighting/

ABOUT OVERVIEW.AI http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing—solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We’re a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We’re growing extremely fast. Our customers love the product because it works—high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).


Why this role is exciting

● Real production ownership: You’re not “support.” You own uptime and line performance where it matters—on the factory floor.


● Hard problems, high signal: Intermittent I/O, timing jitter, noisy signals, flaky networks, multi-trigger edge cases, cycle-time constraints—this is what you’ll solve.


● High learning + high leverage: You’ll deepen elite controls skills while working alongside world-class AI, embedded, and firmware teams to ship permanent fixes into the product.


● Talent density: You’ll be surrounded by fast, intense people who execute. Your work will be taken seriously and acted on quickly.


● High travel, high autonomy: You’ll be trusted as the senior technical owner across multiple sites.


 


WHAT YOU’LL OWN


CONTROLS + LINE INTEGRATION (CORE OF THE JOB)

● Integrate Overview.ai http://Overview.ai systems into automation lines and make them rock-solid.


● Commission and troubleshoot PLC-to-device communication across common industrial protocols:


○ EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, Modbus, OPC UA (and real-world variants)


● Diagnose and eliminate timing and triggering issues:


○ double triggers, missed triggers, trigger bounce, incorrect edge detection, latency/jitter, inconsistent handshakes


● Debug inconsistent network communication:


○ packet loss, unstable links, IP conflicts, switch configuration issues, grounding/noise coupling, cable quality, EMI, misconfigured QoS/VLANs (where applicable)


● Work with PLC logic and machine sequencing to ensure correct system behavior:


○ interpret and modify ladder logic / structured text (as needed) to stabilize integration and performance


● Build repeatable integration standards:


○ wiring patterns, sensor conditioning recommendations, network topologies, validated PLC templates, commissioning checklists



ELECTRICAL + SIGNAL INTEGRITY DEBUGGING (HANDS-ON)

● Troubleshoot sensors, discrete I/O, and signal conditioning in real factory environments:


○ NPN/PNP sensors, pull-ups/pull-downs, filtering/debounce, shielding/grounding, noise mitigation


● Use multimeters and oscilloscopes to diagnose:


○ voltage levels, signal bounce, timing alignment, interference, poor grounding, intermittent connections


● Identify root cause quickly and implement robust fixes—not temporary patches.



FIELD LEADERSHIP + CUSTOMER TRUST

● Be the senior technical point of contact on-site: calm, decisive, and structured under pressure.


● Train customer automation engineers and technicians on best practices for operation and first-line troubleshooting.


● Capture high-quality issue reports for HQ engineering:


○ reproduction steps, logs, electrical measurements, network symptoms, PLC state, and recommended fixes


● Drive feedback loops that result in real product improvements (tooling, diagnostics, reliability features).


 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR


REQUIRED

● Deep PLC / controls engineering experience (you can independently commission and debug automation lines)


● 5+ years in controls / automation / field engineering in high-volume manufacturing environments


● Hands-on expertise with industrial networks and protocols:


○ EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, Modbus, OPC UA (practical troubleshooting, not just theory)


● Strong electrical debugging ability:


○ multimeter + oscilloscope competence, reading schematics, signal condi

source_jobs.title: Technical Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

We're building computer vision software for manufacturing quality control. Our customers are plant directors and quality engineers at manufacturers: They care about reducing defects and shipping faster. Our product proves it works. What we need now is someone who can credibly explain why we're different, earn trust with a technical buyer, and drive a qualified pipeline. That's this role.



What You'll Own:

 - Technical Demos & "Patient Zero" Testing: You are the bridge between engineering and the customer. You will be the first person to test new features in our GTM motions. You will scope, build, and deploy interactive, high-fidelity demos for key industry events, sales pitches, and webinars.

 - Technical Content & Architecture: Write the assets that engineers actually want to read. You'll produce technical deep-dives, API documentation, architectural diagrams, and data-backed case studies. Your job is to translate "our ML model is accurate" into concrete outcomes like "we reduced scrap by 3.2%," partnering directly with our deployments team to pull and analyze real customer telemetry.

 - Growth Engineering & Experiments: Treat our GTM strategy like an engineering problem. You'll design, execute, and measure disciplined experiments across the funnel. This means building internal micro-tools, leveraging APIs for lead enrichment, running A/B tests with strict statistical significance, and writing scripts to automate pipeline generation. If a growth hypothesis fails, you kill it, document the null result, and ship the next iteration.

 - Technical Evangelism & Industry Presence: Be the technical face of our product. Instead of traditional PR, you'll build credibility by speaking at industry events, hosting technical webinars, and engaging directly with communities where our buyers live. You will build the physical and digital demo experiences that make our booth the most crowded at trade shows.

 - GTM Data Infrastructure: Build the systems that let us scale without chaos. You'll own the technical setup of our marketing stack, instrumenting event tracking (e.g., Segment, Mixpanel, HubSpot), building attribution models, and ensuring our GTM data pipeline is clean, accurate, and actionable.

 - Partner Engineering & Competitive Intelligence: Co-create reference architectures and technical integration guides with our hardware partners. You'll tear down competitor products, understand their technical limitations, and build battlecards that arm our sales team with unassailable technical differentiation.



You're a Strong Fit If You Have

 - Engineering Background: A degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent hands-on technical depth. You can read API docs, understand system architecture, and write code (Python, SQL, or similar) to solve growth problems.

 - Technical GTM Experience: 3+ years in Sales Engineering, Developer Advocacy, Technical Product Marketing, or Growth Engineering for a B2B product.

 - A Builder's Portfolio: A track record of things you've built and shipped—whether that's a complex product demo, an open-source contribution, a growth tool you coded, or a deep-dive technical whitepaper (no promotional fluff).

 - Data & Experimentation Chops: You are highly comfortable with data, A/B testing, statistical significance, and instrumenting analytics pipelines. You know what a null result is, and you let the data drive your decisions.

 - A Bias Toward Shipping: You prefer rapid iteration over perfection. You ship four high-impact experiments this quarter, not one perfect campaign.
   

Nice to Have:

 - Familiarity with computer vision, machine learning models, or edge computing deployments.

 - Direct experience selling or marketing industrial, manufacturing, or hardware products to technical buyers.

 - Experience scaling a technical startup's GTM engine from $10M to $100M+ Revenue



We're not looking for someone who:

 - Treats marketing copy as a creative exercise instead of a sale

This is not a cold-start, 0→1 sales role. We already have major customers, executive buy-in, and proven deployments running in real factories today. Your job is to take that momentum and multiply it across factories, regions, and customer sites.

 - Turn warm introductions into live factory projects fast

 - Convert first-station wins into repeatable rollouts across lines and sites

 - Drive urgency and close expansions that don’t “drift to next quarter”

 - Unblock real-world constraints (technical, operational, organizational) that slow velocity

You’ll be selling at the exact moment manufacturing leadership is demanding AI outcomes. This is one of the easiest and most tangible ways for a factory to “enter AI” because it directly improves yield, scrap, throughput, and labor.

We’re supply constrained at times because demand is so strong—the more you’re out in the field, the more you will sell.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

We’re not pre-allocating fixed territories, we’re building them intentionally around talent and opportunity.




1) TERRITORY REVENUE OWNERSHIP (U.S.)

 - Own pipeline, forecasting, and revenue outcomes across your region—typically anchored around where you’re based, with the opportunity to expand beyond it as you grow your accounts

2) TECHNICAL, CONSULTATIVE SELLING (VISION + AUTOMATION)

3) CREATE URGENCY AND REMOVE FRICTION

4) OPERATE LIKE A CLOSER

source_jobs.title: Engineering Intern
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

We build industrial AI cameras used by manufacturers around the world. Our products help customers detect issues in real time and improve manufacturing reliability across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region. Reliability is everything—when our cameras stop working, factory production can be affected.

Our engineering team is based in Taipei, with headquarters in San Francisco. We collaborate closely with software, AI, embedded, and customer engineering teams across multiple regions to build products trusted by manufacturers worldwide.

 
 


THE ROLE

We're looking for an Engineering Intern to join our Taipei engineering team.

This internship focuses on software quality, testing, and automation. You'll start by learning our product through manual end-to-end testing, then gradually take on automated testing, CI workflows, and AI validation tasks. You'll work closely with experienced engineers while gaining hands-on experience building reliable software for real production environments.

This is an excellent opportunity for students interested in software engineering, web development, QA automation, or AI-powered products.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO


MANUAL QA & PRODUCT TESTING

 - Execute end-to-end test cases for new features and product releases.

 - Verify product behavior and reproduce reported issues.

 - Identify edge cases and work with engineers to improve product quality.


TEST AUTOMATION

 - Develop and maintain automated test scripts.

 - Improve testing coverage for new features.

 - Learn how automated testing integrates into our development workflow.


CI & DEVELOPMENT WORKFLOW

 - Help maintain and improve our continuous integration (CI) testing pipeline.

 - Investigate test failures and collaborate with engineers to improve reliability.


AI VALIDATION

 - Validate AI model outputs and product behavior.

 - Help collect and organize testing data to improve our AI feedback loop.


DEVICE SETUP & LAB SUPPORT

 - Prepare cameras and testing devices for software validation.

 - Flash firmware and configure testing environments when needed.

 - Maintain testing equipment and keep the lab organized.

 
 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


REQUIRED

 - Currently pursuing (or recently completed) a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Robotics, or a related technical field.

 - Experience building software projects through coursework, hackathons, or personal projects.

 - Comfortable programming in JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, or another modern programming language.

 - Curious, detail-oriented, and eager to learn.

 - Able to collaborate effectively in a fast-paced engineering environment.

 
 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with JavaScript/TypeScript and modern web development (HTML, CSS, React, etc.).

 - Familiarity with Git and collaborative software development.

 - Experience with testing frameworks such as Playwright, Cypress, Jest, or similar tools.

 - Basic understanding of networking concepts (HTTP, TCP/IP, IP configuration).

 - Familiarity with Linux and command-line tools.

 
 


WHY JOIN US

 - Work on AI products deployed in manufacturing facilities around the world.

 - Learn directly from experienced software, AI, and embedded engineers.

 - Gain hands-on experience with software testing, automation, CI pipelines, and AI systems.

 - Own meaningful projects that directly improve product quality and reliability.

 - Join a fast-growing, profitable startup where interns contribute to real engineering work from day one.

source_jobs.title: Vision Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OVERVIEW.AI http://Overview.ai

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is bringing the cutting edge of AI computer vision to manufacturing—solving inspection problems that were previously not solvable with traditional machine vision. We’re a full-stack company: we deploy GPU-powered cameras on production lines, run inference on the edge, and operate a platform that supports large fleets of devices deployed across the world.

We’re growing extremely fast. Our customers love the product because it works—high accuracy, fast deployment, and an operator-friendly experience that makes real factory rollouts possible (not just pilots).



THE MISSION

Overview.ai http://Overview.ai is building the most powerful edge AI vision platform for manufacturing—deployed on real production lines, not in lab demos. Our AI is only as good as the images we feed it. That’s why this role exists.

We’re hiring a top-tier Vision Applications Engineer in China—someone who can walk up to a difficult inspection problem (reflective metal, transparent plastic, motion blur, awkward geometry, cramped mounting, unstable presentation) and immediately start forming a lighting + optics strategy that makes the problem solvable. You’ll be the person customers remember: the one who makes “impossible” inspections suddenly look obvious.

This role is vision-first. You don’t need to be a controls engineer. You do need to be an exceptional optical/lighting problem solver who can also own field execution: customer communication, on-site deployments, training, and project delivery.




WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING

 - Solve the hardest vision problems in the world: The customers we work with bring problems that break traditional machine vision. You’ll be in the deep end.

 - Work with cutting-edge AI + real factories: You’ll turn great imaging into great AI outcomes—and see the impact immediately on yield and downtime.

 - High autonomy, high trust: You’ll be the expert in the room, leading technical strategy and owning outcomes end-to-end.

 - Win against incumbents: You’ll know the competitive landscape (Cognex, Keyence, HALCON, Hikvision, etc.) and use that knowledge to help us close deals and expand deployments.

 - Travel + variety: New lines, new parts, new weird optical phenomena—this role stays interesting.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL OWN


1) VISION STRATEGY: OPTICS + LIGHTING (CORE OF THE ROLE)

You will design and iterate the imaging solution that makes inspection reliable:

 - Select and tune lighting for challenging surfaces and geometries:
   
   - bright field / dark field, coaxial, dome, diffuse, low-angle, structured light, line lights, spot/point, backlight, multisource setups
   
   - polarization (cross/parallel), glare suppression, specular management
   
   - strobed lighting vs continuous, high-speed exposure strategies, motion-freezing
   
   - “2.5D” techniques: photometric-style approaches, multi-angle illumination, gradient/shape enhancement

 - Select and deploy lenses and optical elements:
   
   - telecentric, macro, high-NA, long working distance, wide-angle vs narrow, Scheimpflug (tilt) where appropriate
   
   - filters (bandpass, long-pass/short-pass), polarizers, diffusers, beam splitters
   
   - mirrors/prisms for impossible mounting angles and constrained spaces

 - Push beyond visible light when needed:
   
   - IR / NIR / SWIR (and other spectral strategies), multispectral approaches, fluorescence-style thinking where relevant

 - Diagnose and solve “weird” vision failure modes:
   
   - reflectivity, transparency, subsurface features, moiré, aliasing, lens flare/ghosting, vibration blur, rolling shutter artifacts, shadowing/occlusion, contamination drift

The bar: you can often tease out a workable imaging approach just by looking at the part + environment, then validate it quickly in the field.




2) PROJECT OWNERSHIP: DEPLOY, STABILIZE, SCALE

 - Own customer deployments end-to-end: planning, on-site execution, training, follow-through.

notes: High-tech manufacturing company with strong focus on AI computer vision and edge-enabled cameras. Rapidly growing with multiple job openings in engineering, sales, and technical roles.
